# Pull Request

## What does this PR do?

<!-- One-sentence summary. -->

## Type of contribution

- [ ] Errata (typo, broken link, citation fix)
- [ ] Case-law update
- [ ] Annotation (in `/annotations/`)
- [ ] Translation (in `/translations/<lang>/`)
- [ ] Companion artefact (in `/companion/`)
- [ ] Bibliography addition
- [ ] Documentation / repository chore
- [ ] Other (please describe)

## Related Issue

<!-- "Closes #12" or "Refs #34" -->

## Citations and sources

<!--
For case-law, statute, or scholarship additions, list the full citations here.
Bluebook (21st ed.), OSCOLA, or APA — pick one and stay consistent.
-->

## Author checklist

- [ ] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) and the [Code of Conduct](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).
- [ ] My contribution is my own original work, or properly cited where it builds on others.
- [ ] I have disclosed any AI assistance used in drafting (model name + scope).
- [ ] I have preserved attribution to the original author and prior contributors.
- [ ] My commit messages follow the convention in CONTRIBUTING.md.
- [ ] For translations: I have read and accepted the translation terms in the issue template.

## AI assistance disclosure (if applicable)

<!--
Using AI to draft, summarise, or translate is fine — passing it off as unaided human scholarship is not.
Example: "Used Claude Opus 4.6 to draft an initial translation of Doctrine III; revised by hand throughout."
-->

## Anything reviewers should know?

<!-- Open questions, judgement calls, areas where you want pushback. -->
